content.verdict: Cocos is a small hillside cluster of brightly-painted wooden cottages between two beaches — Yepton and Valley Church — on Antigua's western coast. Adults-only, all-inclusive, and one of Antigua's best-value honeymoon options, it offers the cottage-with-a-view charm of Cocobay at a lower price point, with the same panoramic Caribbean sunsets and quiet adults atmosphere.
content.best_room: The Cocos Cottages are the standard accommodations — wooden, brightly painted, with private terraces and ocean views from the hillside. The Premium Cottages feature larger terraces and better positioning. The Honeymoon Cottage offers the most secluded position at the top of the property with the most expansive terrace. All cottages share the property's signature Caribbean colour palette and panoramic views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Hillside Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from V.C. Bird International (35 minutes to the western coast between Yepton and Valley Church beaches). Cocos' all-inclusive includes spirits, wines, and most water sports. Welcome drink at the hilltop bar, settle into the cottage with the panoramic view, and dinner at the open-air main restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Two Beaches & Snorkelling
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cocos sits on a headland between two beaches — both calm, swimmer-friendly, and quiet. Morning at one bay with paddleboards, afternoon at the other for snorkelling along the rocky point. Sunset cocktails on the cottage terrace as the sun drops behind the western horizon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive south across the island (40 minutes) to Nelson's Dockyard, the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard from which Admiral Nelson commanded the British fleet. Lunch at the harbourside, walk through the restored 18th-century buildings, and afternoon at Pigeon Point Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive up to Shirley Heights, the 18th-century military lookout above English Harbour, for the iconic sunset over Falmouth Harbour's yachts. Sunday evenings host the legendary steel band sunset gathering with barbecue and rum punch — Antigua's signature social experience.

itinerary_7_nights.title: 365 Beaches Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private driver day visiting several of Antigua's 365 beaches — one for every day of the year. Half Moon Bay on the Atlantic side (surf), Pink Sand Beach, Ffryes Bay (beach bars), and Darkwood Beach. Return to Cocos for dinner with sunset views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat trip to Stingray City off Antigua's north coast — a shallow sandbar where southern stingrays glide around guests in waist-deep water. The trip combines snorkelling at offshore reefs with the stingray swim. Afternoon at Cocos with a kayak paddle along the coast.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Cottage Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last swim at one of the two bays, breakfast on the cottage terrace with the panoramic view, and a 35-minute road transfer back to V.C. Bird International. Most flights depart in the afternoon, leaving time for a slow final morning.

caveats: Cocos is built on a steep hillside — significant walking up and down between cottage and beach. Buggies on request but limited; couples with mobility issues should choose flatter properties.
The cottages are simple wooden construction — charming and atmospheric but rustic in finish. Couples expecting full luxury hotel materials will be surprised.
Cocos is smaller and more low-key than its sister Cocobay — fewer dining options and less programming. The trade-off is more privacy and lower rates.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Cocos is one of Antigua's best honeymoon values — adults-only, all-inclusive, with a stunning hillside cottage setting and panoramic sunsets at moderate rates. The intimate scale and couples-focused atmosphere create a textbook romantic week without the premium pricing of larger luxury properties.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is high season — dry, sunny, breezy. February-March is peak. May, June, and November offer better rates. September-October is hurricane season; the resort sometimes closes for refurbishment.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: For Christmas, New Year, and February-March, book 6-9 months ahead. Premium Cottages and the Honeymoon Cottage sell out first. Shoulder season offers good availability with 2-3 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cocos is strictly adults-only (18+). The intimate scale, hillside layout, and quiet atmosphere make it consistently popular with couples seeking a peaceful adult Caribbean experience.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Premium Cottages for larger terraces and better hill positioning. The Honeymoon Cottage at the top of the property offers the most secluded position with the most expansive view. Standard Cocos Cottages are still genuinely charming at lower rates.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU). Cocos is 35 minutes by road on the western coast between Yepton and Valley Church — the resort arranges private transfers. Direct flights serve Antigua from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, Toronto, and major European hubs.
